name: e2b

metadata.name: e2b
metadata.summary: E2B SDK that give agents cloud environments

documentation: ## What is E2B? [E2B](https://www.e2b.dev/) is an open-source infrastructure that allows you run to AI-generated code in secure isolated sandboxes in the cloud. To start and control sandboxes, use our [JavaScript SDK](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@e2b/code-interpreter) or [Python SDK](https://pypi.org/project/e2b_code_interpreter). ## Run your first Sandbox ### 1. Install SDK ``` pip install e2b-code-interpreter ``` ### 2. Get your E2B API key 1. Sign up to E2B [here](https://e2b.dev). 2. Get your API key [here](https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=keys). 3. Set environment variable with your API key ``` E2B_API_KEY=e2b_*** ``` ### 3. Execute code with code interpreter inside Sandbox ```py from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox with Sandbox() as sandbox: sandbox.run_code("x = 1") execution = sandbox.run_code("x+=1; x") print(execution.text) # outputs 2 ``` ### 4. Check docs Visit [E2B documentation](https://e2b.dev/docs). ### 5. E2B cookbook Visit our [Cookbook](https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b-cookbook/tree/main) to get inspired by examples with different LLMs and AI frameworks.

documentation: Use `AsyncSandbox.create()` to create a new sandbox instead.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.main.AsyncSandbox.is_running
documentation:
Check if the sandbox is running.

```
param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds

return

 if the sandbox is running,  otherwise
```

Example
python
sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create()
await sandbox.is_running() # Returns True

await sandbox.kill()
await sandbox.is_running() # Returns False

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.main.AsyncSandbox.create
documentation:
Create a new sandbox.

By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

```
param template

Sandbox template name or ID

param timeout

Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

param metadata

Custom metadata for the sandbox

param envs

Custom environment variables for the sandbox

param api_key

E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to  environment variable

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds

return

sandbox instance for the new sandbox
```

Use this method instead of using the constructor to create a new sandbox.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.main.AsyncSandbox.connect
documentation:
Connect to an existing sandbox.
With sandbox ID you can connect to the same sandbox from different places or environments (serverless functions, etc).

```
param sandbox_id

Sandbox ID

param api_key

E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to  environment variable

return

sandbox instance for the existing sandbox
```

@example
python
sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create()
sandbox_id = sandbox.sandbox_id

\# Another code block
same_sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.connect(sandbox_id)

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.main.AsyncSandbox.set_timeout
documentation:
Set the timeout of the sandbox.
After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

```
param timeout

Timeout for the sandbox in seconds

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.main.AsyncSandbox.set_timeout
documentation:
Set the timeout of the specified sandbox.
After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

```
param sandbox_id

Sandbox ID

param timeout

Timeout for the sandbox in seconds

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds
```

E2B cloud sandbox is a secure and isolated cloud environment.

The sandbox allows you to:
- Access Linux OS
- Create, list, and delete files and directories
- Run commands
- Run isolated code
- Access the internet

Check docs [here](https://e2b.dev/docs).

Use the `AsyncSandbox.create()` to create a new sandbox.

Example:
```python
from e2b import AsyncSandbox

sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create()
```

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.SandboxException
documentation:
Base class for all sandbox errors.

Raised when a general sandbox exception occurs.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.TimeoutException
documentation:
Raised when a timeout occurs.

The [unavailable] exception type is caused by sandbox timeout.

The [canceled] exception type is caused by exceeding request timeout.

The [deadline_exceeded] exception type is caused by exceeding the timeout for process, watch, etc.

The [unknown] exception type is sometimes caused by the sandbox timeout when the request is not processed correctly.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.InvalidArgumentException
documentation: Raised when an invalid argument is provided.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.NotEnoughSpaceException
documentation: Raised when there is not enough disk space.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.NotFoundException
documentation: Raised when a resource is not found.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.AuthenticationException
documentation: Raised when authentication fails.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.TemplateException
documentation: Exception raised when the template uses old envd version. It isn't compatible with the new SDK.

classes.name: e2b.exceptions.RateLimitException
documentation: Raised when the API rate limit is exceeded.

modules.name: e2b
documentation:
Secure sandboxed cloud environments made for AI agents and AI apps.

Check docs [here](https://e2b.dev/docs).

E2B Sandbox is a secure cloud sandbox environment made for AI agents and AI
apps.
Sandboxes allow AI agents and apps to have long running cloud secure environments.
In these environments, large language models can use the same tools as humans do.

E2B Python SDK supports both sync and async API:

```py
from e2b import Sandbox

# Create sandbox
sandbox = Sandbox()
```

```py
from e2b import AsyncSandbox

# Create sandbox
sandbox = await AsyncSandbox.create()
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.commands.command_handle.AsyncCommandHandle.exit_code
documentation:
Command execution exit code.

`0` if the command finished successfully.

It is `None` if the command is still running.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.commands.command_handle.AsyncCommandHandle.disconnect
documentation:
Disconnects from the command.

The command is not killed, but SDK stops receiving events from the command.
You can reconnect to the command using `sandbox.commands.connect` method.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.commands.command_handle.AsyncCommandHandle.wait
documentation:
Wait for the command to finish and return the result.
If the command exits with a non-zero exit code, it throws a `CommandExitException`.

```
return

 result of command execution
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.commands.command_handle.AsyncCommandHandle.kill
documentation:
Kills the command.

It uses `SIGKILL` signal to kill the command

```
return

 if the command was killed successfully,  if the command was not found
```

Command execution handle.

It provides methods for waiting for the command to finish, retrieving stdout/stderr, and killing the command.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_async.filesystem.filesystem.Filesystem.write
documentation:
Write content to a file on the path.

Writing to a file that doesn't exist creates the file.

Writing to a file that already exists overwrites the file.

Writing to a file at path that doesn't exist creates the necessary directories.

```
param path

Path to the file

param data

Data to write to the file, can be a , , or .

param user

Run the operation as this user

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds

return

```

functions.name: e2b.api.client.api.sandboxes.post_sandboxes_sandbox_id_timeout.sync_detailed
documentation:
Set the timeout for the sandbox. The sandbox will expire x seconds from the time of the request.
Calling this method multiple times overwrites the TTL, each time using the current timestamp as the
starting point to measure the timeout duration.

Args:
    sandbox_id (str):
    body (PostSandboxesSandboxIDTimeoutBody):

Raises:
    errors.UnexpectedStatus: If the server returns an undocumented status code and Client.raise_on_unexpected_status is True.

Returns:

methods.name: e2b.api.client.client.Client.__aenter__
documentation: Enter a context manager for underlying httpx.AsyncClient—you cannot enter twice (see httpx docs)

methods.name: e2b.api.client.client.Client.__aexit__
documentation: Exit a context manager for underlying httpx.AsyncClient (see httpx docs)

A class for keeping track of data related to the API

The following are accepted as keyword arguments and will be used to construct httpx Clients internally:

> `base_url`: The base URL for the API, all requests are made to a relative path to this URL
>
> `cookies`: A dictionary of cookies to be sent with every request
>
> `headers`: A dictionary of headers to be sent with every request
>
> `timeout`: The maximum amount of a time a request can take. API functions will raise
> httpx.TimeoutException if this is exceeded.
>
> `verify_ssl`: Whether or not to verify the SSL certificate of the API server. This should be True in production,
> but can be set to False for testing purposes.
>
> `follow_redirects`: Whether or not to follow redirects. Default value is False.
>
> `httpx_args`: A dictionary of additional arguments to be passed to the `httpx.Client` and `httpx.AsyncClient` constructor.

Attributes:
: raise_on_unexpected_status: Whether or not to raise an errors.UnexpectedStatus if the API returns a

  : status code that was not documented in the source OpenAPI document. Can also be provided as a keyword
    argument to the constructor.

documentation: Get the underlying httpx.AsyncClient, constructing a new one if not previously set

methods.name: e2b.api.client.client.AuthenticatedClient.__aenter__
documentation: Enter a context manager for underlying httpx.AsyncClient—you cannot enter twice (see httpx docs)

methods.name: e2b.api.client.client.AuthenticatedClient.__aexit__
documentation: Exit a context manager for underlying httpx.AsyncClient (see httpx docs)

A Client which has been authenticated for use on secured endpoints

The following are accepted as keyword arguments and will be used to construct httpx Clients internally:

> `base_url`: The base URL for the API, all requests are made to a relative path to this URL
>
> `cookies`: A dictionary of cookies to be sent with every request
>
> `headers`: A dictionary of headers to be sent with every request
>
> `timeout`: The maximum amount of a time a request can take. API functions will raise
> httpx.TimeoutException if this is exceeded.
>
> `verify_ssl`: Whether or not to verify the SSL certificate of the API server. This should be True in production,
> but can be set to False for testing purposes.
>
> `follow_redirects`: Whether or not to follow redirects. Default value is False.
>
> `httpx_args`: A dictionary of additional arguments to be passed to the `httpx.Client` and `httpx.AsyncClient` constructor.

Attributes:

: raise_on_unexpected_status: Whether or not to raise an errors.UnexpectedStatus if the API returns a

  : status code that was not documented in the source OpenAPI document. Can also be provided as a keyword
    argument to the constructor.

  token: The token to use for authentication
  prefix: The prefix to use for the Authorization header
  auth_header_name: The name of the Authorization header

methods.name: e2b.sandbox.main.SandboxSetup.download_url
documentation:
Get the URL to download a file from the sandbox.

```
param path

Path to the file to download

return

URL for downloading file
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox.main.SandboxSetup.upload_url
documentation:
Get the URL to upload a file to the sandbox.

You have to send a POST request to this URL with the file as multipart/form-data.

```
param path

Path to the file to upload

return

URL for uploading file
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox.main.SandboxSetup.get_host
documentation:
Get the host address to connect to the sandbox.
You can then use this address to connect to the sandbox port from outside the sandbox via HTTP or WebSocket.

```
param port

Port to connect to

return

Host address to connect to
```

classes.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.filesystem.watch_handle.WatchHandle

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.filesystem.watch_handle.WatchHandle.stop
documentation:
Stop watching the directory.
After you stop the watcher you won't be able to get the events anymore.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.filesystem.watch_handle.WatchHandle.get_new_events
documentation:
Get the latest events that have occurred in the watched directory since the last call, or from the beginning of the watching, up until now.

```
return

List of filesystem events
```

Handle for watching filesystem events.
It is used to get the latest events that have occurred in the watched directory.

Use `.stop()` to stop watching the directory.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.sandbox_api.SandboxApi.list
documentation:
List all running sandboxes.

```
param api_key

API key to use for authentication, defaults to  environment variable

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.main.Sandbox.__init__
documentation:
Create a new sandbox.

By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

```
param template

Sandbox template name or ID

param timeout

Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users

param metadata

Custom metadata for the sandbox

param envs

Custom environment variables for the sandbox

param api_key

E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to  environment variable

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds

return

sandbox instance for the new sandbox
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.main.Sandbox.is_running
documentation:
Check if the sandbox is running.

```
param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds

return

 if the sandbox is running,  otherwise
```

Example
python
sandbox = Sandbox()
sandbox.is_running() # Returns True

sandbox.kill()
sandbox.is_running() # Returns False

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.main.Sandbox.connect
documentation:
Connects to an existing Sandbox.
With sandbox ID you can connect to the same sandbox from different places or environments (serverless functions, etc).

```
param sandbox_id

Sandbox ID

param api_key

E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to  environment variable

return

sandbox instance for the existing sandbox
```

@example
python
sandbox = Sandbox()
sandbox_id = sandbox.sandbox_id

\# Another code block
same_sandbox = Sandbox.connect(sandbox_id)

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.main.Sandbox.set_timeout
documentation:
Set the timeout of the sandbox.
After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

```
param timeout

Timeout for the sandbox in seconds

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.main.Sandbox.set_timeout
documentation:
Set the timeout of the sandbox specified by sandbox ID.
After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

```
param sandbox_id

Sandbox ID

param timeout

Timeout for the sandbox in seconds

param api_key

E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to  environment variable

param request_timeout

Timeout for the request in seconds
```

E2B cloud sandbox is a secure and isolated cloud environment.

The sandbox allows you to:
- Access Linux OS
- Create, list, and delete files and directories
- Run commands
- Run isolated code
- Access the internet

Check docs [here](https://e2b.dev/docs).

Use the `Sandbox()` to create a new sandbox.

Example:
```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox()
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.filesystem.filesystem.Filesystem.write
documentation:
Write content to a file on the path.

Writing to a file that doesn't exist creates the file.

Writing to a file that already exists overwrites the file.

Writing to a file at path that doesn't exist creates the necessary directories.

```
param path

Path to the file

param data

Data to write to the file, can be a , , or .

param user

Run the operation as this user

param request_timeout
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.commands.command_handle.CommandHandle.disconnect
documentation:
Disconnect from the command.

The command is not killed, but SDK stops receiving events from the command.
You can reconnect to the command using `sandbox.commands.connect` method.

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.commands.command_handle.CommandHandle.wait
documentation:
Wait for the command to finish and returns the result.
If the command exits with a non-zero exit code, it throws a `CommandExitException`.

```
param on_pty

Callback for pty output

param on_stdout

Callback for stdout output

param on_stderr

Callback for stderr output

return

 result of command execution
```

methods.name: e2b.sandbox_sync.commands.command_handle.CommandHandle.kill
documentation:
Kills the command.

It uses `SIGKILL` signal to kill the command.

```
return

Whether the command was killed successfully
```

Command execution handle.

It provides methods for waiting for the command to finish, retrieving stdout/stderr, and killing the command.
